policy.title: RBI finalizes phased climate risk disclosure norms for SCBs
policy.description: RBI released final guidelines requiring scheduled commercial banks to disclose Scope 1-2 emissions by FY27 and integrate climate stress testing into ICAAP by FY28. Banks with assets above ₹10 lakh crore must comply by April 2027; others get a 12-month extension.

policy.title: SEBI mandates BRSR Core assurance for top 500 listed companies
policy.description: SEBI extended mandatory limited assurance on BRSR Core metrics to the top 500 companies by market capitalization, effective FY27. Value chain Scope 3 disclosure becomes mandatory for top 250 companies from FY28.

policy.title: IRDAI issues master direction on index-based parametric covers
policy.description: IRDAI published comprehensive guidelines for parametric insurance products, permitting insurers to use satellite and IoT-based triggers for agriculture, property, and SME covers. Products require pre-approval but benefit from expedited 30-day review.

policy.title: Basel Committee publishes final Pillar 3 climate disclosure standard
policy.description: BCBS finalized Pillar 3 climate disclosure requirements covering transition and physical risk exposures, with G-SIBs required to comply by January 2028. The framework includes standardized templates for financed emissions in high-carbon sectors.

policy.title: ISSB releases implementation guidance for emerging market jurisdictions
policy.description: IFRS Foundation published tailored guidance for ISSB S1/S2 adoption in emerging markets, addressing data gaps, proportionality thresholds, and phased timelines. India is among 12 jurisdictions flagged for accelerated adoption pathways.

policy.title: NGFS releases Phase V scenarios with enhanced South Asia granularity
policy.description: NGFS updated its climate scenarios to include sub-national physical risk projections for India covering flood, heat stress, and water scarcity through 2100. The scenarios now offer district-level damage functions for financial sector stress testing.

competitors.title: Jupiter partners with HDFC Bank for physical risk analytics
competitors.description: Jupiter Intelligence announced a strategic partnership with HDFC Bank to deploy its ClimateScore platform across the bank's commercial real estate and infrastructure lending portfolios. The multi-year engagement covers 15 Indian states.

competitors.title: Sprih raises $18M Series B for supply chain ESG expansion
competitors.description: Bangalore-based Sprih closed an $18 million Series B led by Accel and Elevar Equity to scale its supply chain sustainability platform across South Asia. The company claims 400+ enterprise clients and plans BRSR reporting automation features.

competitors.title: Moody's launches India-specific physical risk scores for banks
competitors.description: Moody's ESG Solutions introduced a physical climate risk scoring module tailored to Indian banks, covering asset-level flood, cyclone, and heat exposure. The product integrates with Moody's credit risk models for PD adjustments.

competitors.title: XDI releases India infrastructure risk database with CRISIL
competitors.description: XDI partnered with CRISIL to launch a granular physical risk database covering 50,000+ Indian infrastructure assets. The dataset includes projected damage costs under multiple NGFS scenarios through 2080.

competitors.title: Watershed adds BRSR and ISSB reporting modules for Indian market
competitors.description: Watershed expanded its enterprise carbon platform with dedicated modules for SEBI BRSR and ISSB S2 compliance, targeting Indian multinationals and their global subsidiaries. The launch includes automated Scope 3 calculation for 12 sectors.

competitors.title: GIST Advisory selected by NABARD for climate-smart agriculture metrics
competitors.description: GIST Advisory won a contract with NABARD to develop natural capital accounting frameworks for climate-smart agriculture lending. The engagement covers impact measurement for ₹25,000 crore in priority sector advances.

research.title: Climate Risk and Financial Stability in South Asia: A Stress Testing Framework
research.description: The report finds that Indian banks face potential NPL increases of 2.8-4.1 percentage points under acute flood and heat scenarios by 2035. It proposes a standardized stress testing methodology calibrated to South Asian hazard profiles.

research.title: Scaling Parametric Insurance in Emerging Markets: Lessons from India
research.description: Swiss Re estimates India's parametric insurance market could reach $2.5 billion in premiums by 2030, driven by MSME and agriculture uptake. The study identifies basis risk calibration and regulatory clarity as key adoption barriers.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk in Credit Portfolios: Methodological Advances
research.description: BIS working paper presents improved methodologies for translating physical hazard data into PD and LGD adjustments, finding that traditional credit models underestimate climate-driven defaults by 35-60% in high-exposure sectors.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Pathways to Comparability
research.description: IFC analysis reveals that only 23% of listed companies in South Asia provide decision-useful climate metrics, compared to 67% in developed markets. The report recommends proxy methodologies and satellite-derived indicators to fill gaps.

research.title: Climate Risk Assessment for Indian Banking Sector
research.description: RBI's inaugural climate risk report estimates that 12% of scheduled commercial bank exposures are in high physical risk districts. The study identifies agriculture, textiles, and real estate as sectors requiring enhanced due diligence.

research.title: Climate Insurance Protection Gap: India Country Assessment
research.description: Munich Re calculates India's climate insurance protection gap at $48 billion annually, with flood and cyclone perils accounting for 65% of uninsured losses. The assessment highlights SME and agricultural segments as priority intervention areas.

ourRead.headline: India's climate finance infrastructure is crystallizing fast—StepChange must cement its position as the bridge between regulatory mandates and institutional execution.
body: The past 60 days mark an inflection point: India's climate risk disclosure regime is no longer aspirational—it's operational. RBI's finalized climate disclosure norms, SEBI's BRSR assurance expansion, and IRDAI's parametric insurance guidelines collectively signal that financial institutions face hard compliance deadlines within 12-18 months. Global standards from Basel and NGFS are converging with domestic requirements, creating a narrow window for data and analytics providers to establish themselves as essential infrastructure.

RBI's April 18 disclosure framework is the most consequential development for StepChange's core business. The mandate that banks integrate climate stress testing into ICAAP by FY28—combined with NGFS's new district-level damage functions for India—creates immediate demand for precisely what we build: hazard-to-PD translation models. Banks above ₹10 lakh crore face April 2027 deadlines, meaning procurement cycles are starting now. The BIS working paper finding that traditional models underestimate climate defaults by 35-60% gives us the technical ammunition to justify our methodology to risk committees.

The competitor landscape is intensifying, but with revealing fault lines. Jupiter's HDFC partnership and Moody's India-specific product launch confirm that global players are prioritizing the Indian market—but both are positioning as bolt-on analytics rather than embedded infrastructure. More concerning is XDI's CRISIL partnership, which directly competes with our data layer. However, Sprih's fundraise and Watershed's BRSR module show that most competitors remain focused on disclosure software rather than risk quantification. StepChange's differentiation must be ruthless: we are not a reporting tool; we are the risk intelligence engine that makes reporting possible.

The Swiss Re and Munich Re reports on parametric insurance present a strategic opportunity beyond our current positioning. Swiss Re's $2.5 billion market estimate by 2030 and IRDAI's new expedited approval pathway validate our parametric insurance bet—but execution requires partnerships with insurers who lack hazard calibration expertise. The IFC's finding that only 23% of South Asian companies provide decision-useful climate metrics is equally actionable: our proprietary ESG datasets can fill exactly these gaps for banks struggling with Scope 3 and supply chain due diligence requirements.

Watch: Jupiter's HDFC engagement—if it expands beyond CRE to working capital lending, they become a direct threat. Action: Fast-track a pilot proposal to at least two PSU banks citing the RBI ICAAP deadline; use the BIS underestimation finding as the lead message. Time-to-mandate is now our best sales argument.
